name: Bard Quartet
description: Quad programmable quantizer with integrated keyboard interface. Four channels, 8 harmonies, 10 memory slots. Supports microtuning, arpeggiator, channel grouping, and harmony progressions for full song structures.

controls.name: Keyboard Buttons
controls.description: 12 keyboard buttons to set notes per scale per channel. Active note shines brighter for real-time note monitoring.
controls.detail: One octave of chromatic keys · C# and D# = Load/Save in Memory menu

controls.name: Harmony Potentiometer
controls.description: Navigates between 8 different harmony locations for the active channel, enabling harmonic progressions. Also used for microtuning (+/-50 cents).
controls.detail: 8 harmony positions per channel · ±50 cents in microtuning mode

controls.name: Channel Buttons
controls.description: Select the active channel being edited (1–4). Hold + another channel button to create a group that shares scale settings.
controls.detail: 4 channel buttons · grouped channels share LED illumination

controls.name: Edit Button
controls.description: Opens Edit menu for Octave setting, Trigger Out assignment, Gate In assignment, and Transpose mode per channel and harmony.
controls.detail: Octave: -3 to +3 · hold 2s for Tuning Mode output

controls.name: µTune / Option Button
controls.description: Opens microtuning menu to detune any note ±50 cents. Also used with Edit to access Options menu. Long press with Harmony pot dissociates pot from current harmony.
controls.detail: ±50 cents per note · Edit+µTune = Options menu

controls.name: ARPG / Memory Button
controls.description: Opens Arpeggiator menu (hold channel button to enable arp per channel). With Edit = Memory menu for saving/loading 10 slots.
controls.detail: Arpeggiator modes: Up / Down / Pendulum / Spiral / Random · 1–4 octave spread

inputs.name: IN 1–4 (Channel CV)
inputs.description: V/Oct CV inputs for each of the four quantizer channels.

inputs.name: TRSP (Transpose CV)
inputs.description: Transpose CV input. Can work as pre-quantize, post-quantize, or octave transposer per channel (set in Edit menu).

inputs.name: Gate Input
inputs.description: Gate input used as Track & Hold (default) or Sample & Hold (selectable in Options). Assigned per channel in Edit menu.

inputs.name: Harmony CV
inputs.description: CV input for Harmony potentiometer position, selecting between the 8 harmony locations.

outputs.name: OUT 1–4 (Channel CV)
outputs.description: Quantized V/Oct outputs for each channel. Supports V/Oct, Hz/V, and 1.2V/Oct output norms.

outputs.name: HRMN (Harmony CV)
outputs.description: Harmony CV output mirroring the current harmony position for use with other modules.

outputs.name: TRIG OUT
outputs.description: Trigger output that fires each time a note change is detected on an assigned channel.

behaviors.name: Scale Programming
behaviors.description: Use keyboard buttons to activate/deactivate notes per channel per harmony. Up to 320 scales storable (4 channels × 8 harmonies × 10 memory slots).

behaviors.name: Harmony Progression
behaviors.description: 8 independent harmony settings per channel. Navigate via pot or CV to create chord progressions and song structures. Changes also affect Edit, Arp, microtuning, and grouping.

behaviors.name: Arpeggiator
behaviors.description: Each channel can become an arpeggiator driven by triggers at its CV input instead of continuous voltage. Modes: Up, Down, Pendulum, Spiral, Random. 1–4 octave spread per channel and harmony.

behaviors.name: Microtuning
behaviors.description: Each note in each channel and harmony can be detuned ±50 cents independently. Tuning setting is stored per harmony. Harmony pot navigates freely when µTune held.

behaviors.name: Channel Grouping
behaviors.description: Grouped channels share scale settings (and optionally Edit, Microtuning, Arpeggiator settings per Options menu). Indicated by simultaneous LED illumination.

behaviors.name: Channel Interaction (Normalization / Shift Register)
behaviors.description: When 0V detected at a channel input, G#=Normalization (follow previous channel), A#=Shift Register (follow previous on gate trigger). Enables cascading voice effects.

patchIdeas.name: Harmonic progression sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: LFO or sequencer → HRMN CV · all 4 channels play different scale roles · Harmony pot advances through 8 chord positions

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic arp
patchIdeas.patch: 4 channels as arpeggiatores · clock → all CV inputs · different scales per channel · TRSP for transposition

patchIdeas.name: Shift register melody
patchIdeas.patch: A# option ON for channels 2-4 · gate sequence → Gate input · channel 1 plays melody · channels 2-4 echo it with delay
